description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages prepare, check, test, and report, using a Composer cache at ~/.composer keyed by ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}_${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}. Add a prepare_cache job in the prepare stage that runs `composer validate` and `composer install`, pulls and pushes a vendor/ cache (key ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}) and stores the vendor/* files as a 2‑hour artifact named vendor. In the check stage define code-style (needs prepare_cache) running `vendor/bin/ecs`, lint running `find src/ -type f -name '*.php' -exec php -l {} \; | (! grep -v "No syntax errors detected")`, mlc using image registry.gitlab.com/tjvb/tools-images/mlc running `mlc doc`, phpmd running `composer phpmd`, phpstan running `php -d memory_limit=500M vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --error-format=gitlab | tee phpstan.json` and `cat phpstan.json`, infection (image php83) running `composer install`, `vendor/bin/phpunit --migrate-configuration`, `vendor/bin/infection`, and vale (image registry.gitlab.com/tjvb/tools-images/vale with empty entrypoint) running `/bin/vale --config=tools/vale/.vale.ini README.md CONTRIBUTING.md`; all these jobs depend on and need prepare_cache and publish appropriate artifacts (codequality reports, infection build, phpstan.json). In the test stage add a matrix test job (image php$PHP) that for each Laravel/PHP/Testbench combination echoes the Laravel version, runs `composer require --dev "orchestra/testbench=^$TESTBENCH"`, executes `vendor/bin/phpunit --migrate-configuration || true`, then runs `XDEBUG_MODE=coverage vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-text --colors=never --coverage-cobertura=phpunitresult/cobertura-coverage.xml --log-junit=phpunitresult/junit.xml`, and finally rewrites the coverage XML with `sed -i 's~ filename="~ filename="src/~' phpunitresult/cobertura-coverage.xml`; also add a test_lowest job (image php80) that runs `composer update --prefer-lowest` and the same phpunit coverage commands, both producing coverage and JUnit artifacts. All jobs reference the global cache, appropriate dependencies, and no special rules or deployment targets are defined.
